topic: Kernel-verified Lean proof closing an open sorry in Erdős Problem 829 (formal-conjectures)

artifacts.title: Lean 4 proof of Erdos829.sumRep_cubes_three (3 is not an ordered sum of two cubes), kernel-verified

metadata.method: sumRep as filtered-antidiagonal cardinality; k ≤ k^3 bounds rule out 2,3 as cubes; the four pairs (a,b) with a+b=3 each contain a 2 or 3

candidate_claims.assertion: The Lean 4 theorem `Erdos829.sumRep_cubes_three` is proven and kernel-verified (axioms: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound; no sorryAx): the number of ordered representations of 3 as a sum of two perfect cubes is 0, i.e. 3 is not the sum of two cubes. Closes a previously-open `sorry` (category: test) in google-deepmind/formal-conjectures Erdős Problem 829.
caveats: A small computational fact (a `test`-category supporting lemma), not the open Erdős 829 conjecture itself.
Kernel-verified locally; upstream PR is the external acceptance step and requires the maintainer's action.
